reason: Bitcoin is currently in a markdown phase, having failed to hold critical support levels of $70,000 and $68,000. Macroeconomic factors, such as a strengthening dollar, are exerting downward pressure, while news of a BlackRock fund issue further impacts crypto and DeFi markets. An investment firm's prediction of a potential 30% crash underscores the significant downside risk and bearish sentiment prevailing in the market.
headlines: Bitcoin slips below $68,000 heading into the weekend as dollar posts steepest weekly gain in a year
Bitcoin could crash by another 30% as four-year cycle gains strength, investment firm says
Why bitcoin couldn't hold $70,000 despite its best week of Wall Street news in months
Managing financial AI agents is the only skill you’ll need to survive the AI layoffs
BlackRock private credit fund is latest to crack, hitting crypto prices and DeFi markets
